articles.title: Retailers' Sales Growth Cooled Last Month
articles.excerpt: Sales growth slowed for U.S. retailers in April, damped by a cooler increase in gasoline prices as the Iran conflict continued.

articles.title: Trump-Xi summit could hinge on these two crucial tech flashpoints
articles.excerpt: Critical minerals and market access for U.S. tech companies could be key points of discussion at the Trump-Xi summit. Trump commented in a Truth Social post earlier this week that opening up China for U.S. businesses would be his "first request" to Xi.

articles.title: Miran Says a Transition at the Fed Is Important
articles.excerpt: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran says outgoing Chair Jerome Powell could help create a smooth transition with his successor, Kevin Warsh, by staying on the central bank's Board of governors. Powell is staying on the board after his term expires Friday.

articles.title: The Stock Market Is Growing Increasingly Dependent On The Stock Market
articles.excerpt: Mega-cap tech stocks are driving S&P 500 highs despite rising rates, inflation, and economic headwinds. Corporate profit growth and AI-driven productivity are offsetting input cost pressures, sustaining near-record margins.

articles.title: A Fact Check of Trump's Claims on Inflation and Gas Prices in Iran War
articles.excerpt: He has minimized soaring gas prices, rising inflation and the American economy's need for the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Fed's Miran on Supply Shocks, Policy, Central Bank Tenure
articles.excerpt: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran speaks about inflation from supply shocks, how the central bank can work with the Treasury and White House going forward, and reflects on his time at the Fed. -------- More on Bloomberg Television and Markets Like this video?

articles.title: U.S Jobless Claims Rose Last Week
articles.excerpt: U.S. jobless claims rose last week but were still relatively low, signaling employers held back from widespread layoffs.

articles.title: U.S. retail sales increase again, but higher gas prices and inflation played a big role
articles.excerpt: Sales at retailers rose in April for the third month in a row, but the increase stemmed in large part from drivers spending more on gasoline because of higher prices. Spending was on the softer side after adjusting for inflation.

articles.title: Trump Brought An Army Of CEOs To Beijing For A Reason
articles.excerpt: President Trump's China trip aims to secure rare earth material access, with major tech, industrial, and finance executives present to facilitate negotiations. Key negotiation topics include AI chips, rare earths, tariffs, and possible large purchase agreements benefiting firms l

articles.title: Are Large Caps Really Leading This Rally? A Market Breadth Study
articles.excerpt: Breadth has been a hot topic during the stock rally over the last six weeks.

articles.title: Nasdaq and S&P set for new records as Trump talks up US-China relations
articles.excerpt: US markets were set for another higher open on Thursday despite mounting inflation concerns and rising Treasury yields, as investors continued piling into artificial intelligence-linked technology stocks. Dow Jones futures indicated an initial gain of 377 points, or 0.8%, while S

articles.title: Meet The 17th Chair Of The Federal Reserve
articles.excerpt: Following the closest Senate vote since the confirmation requirement began in 1977, Kevin Warsh has officially been cleared to become the next chair of the Federal Reserve. Under his coming leadership, Warsh has vowed that monetary policy will remain "strictly independent" and ha

articles.title: Bank of Spain calls for access to advanced AI tools, flags cyber risks
articles.excerpt: The Bank of Spain on Thursday called for stronger international cooperation and wider access to ​protective artificial intelligence models such as Anthropic's Glasswing ‌to ward off cybersecurity risks stemming from the potential misuse of advanced vulnerability-spotting engines.

articles.title: Talks to avert strike at Inpex's Australia LNG plant enter final day
articles.excerpt: Talks to avert industrial action at Inpex's Ichthys liquefied natural gas facility in Australia enter their final day on ​Friday, with unions warning they will issue a strike ‌notice unless a deal is reached.

articles.title: U.S. Stock Futures Tick Up With AI Trade Top of Mind Again
articles.excerpt: Futures contracts for the main U.S. indexes were building on gains from the previous session as the tech stock rally continues.

articles.title: Tech Can Crown the Next Superpower as Trump and China Face Off
articles.excerpt: Cisco beats expectations, Micron tops $900 billion valuation, Fervo Energy makes its market debut, and more news to start your day.

articles.title: The Bond Market Is Breaking, Stocks To Follow
articles.excerpt: We are facing an inflationary shock, and the Federal Funds futures are starting to price a Fed hike. The 10Y yields are rising driven by rising inflation expectations, currently at the edge of de-anchoring.

articles.title: The Stock Market's Fear Gauge Is Faulty
articles.excerpt: Plus, Trump in China pumps up tech stocks.

articles.title: What the biggest mid-cycle earnings acceleration on record means for the stock market
articles.excerpt: A trader looks back to figure out why he missed the stock-market surge from late March.

articles.title: Dow futures surge 250 points: 5 things to know before market opens
articles.excerpt: US stock futures edged higher on Wednesday after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 closed at fresh record highs, extending a rally led by large technology stocks. S&P 500 futures rose 0.13%, Nasdaq 100 futures gained 0.07%, and Dow futures climbed 260 points, or 0.41%.
